title: Sliding roof for a vehicle
abstract: A sliding roof comprises at least a first panel and a second panel. The panel are slidably provided on one or two roof rails. At one end of the rail, a cassette is provided. The cassette comprises pairs of substantially parallel cassette rails located above one another. The cassette is movable to ahgn each pair of cassette rails with the roof rails for receiving the rails. The roof panels are slid into the cassette and accommodated by the cassette rails, above one another. The cassette may be lowered to fit, for example, under a front deck of a ship. The panels are connected and, during sliding, forced at a distance from one another to slide over a curved track without colliding. While connected, only one of the panels is connected to a push/pull cable that is driven by a pinion or similar circular device.
claims:
Claims Claims Sliding roof system for a vehicle, the system comprising: A first roof rail having a distal end and a proximal end; A first roof panel slidably mounted to the first roof rail, in closed state of the roof substantially ranging to the distal end; A second roof panel slidably mounted to the first roof rail, in closed state of the roof substantially ranging to the proximal end; A panel storage module having a first panel storage position and a second panel storage position, each storage position comprising a first storage rail for slidably receiving a roof panel; A storage driving module arranged for moving the panel storage module between a first storage position in which the first storage rail of the first panel storage position is lined up to the proximal end of the first roof rail and a second storage position in which the first storage rail of the second panel storage position is lined up to the proximal end of the first roof rail; and A panel driving module for driving the first roof panel and the second roof panel along the first roof rail and the first storage rails. Sliding roof system according to claim 1, wherein the storage driving module is further arranged for moving the panel storage module to a third storage position in which the first storage position and the second storage position are located below the proximal end of the first roof rail. Sliding roof system according to claim 1 or 2, wherein a first of the first roof panel and the second roof panel comprises a spacer 

 module for forcing a space between the first roof panel and the second roof panel. Sliding roof system according to claim 3, the spacer module comprising a spring loaded piston, the spring being arranged to force the piston outward and away from the first of the first roof panel and the second roof panel Sliding roof system according to claim 3 or 4, further comprising a panel drive control module arranged to control the panel driving module to move a first of the first roof panel and the second roof panel away from a second of the first of the first panel and the second panel prior to moving the first roof panel and the second roof panel to the storage positions. 6. Shding roof system according to claim 5, wherein the control module is further arranged to control the panel driving module to move a first of the first roof panel and the second roof panel towards a second of the first of the first roof panel and the second roof panel after moving the first roof panel and the second roof panel to their positions for closing the roof. 7. Sliding roof system according to any of the preceding claim, the first roof panel comprising a first connector of a first type arranged to engage with a first connector of a second type comprised by the second panel for coupling the first roof panel to the second roof panel. 

 Sliding roof system according to claim 7, wherein the panel driving module is connected to the first roof panel for driving the first roof panel and the second roof panel. Sliding roof system according to claim 7 or 8, wherein: The first connector comprises a protrusion having a directional component perpendicular to the roof trajectory of the roof panels along the first roof rail; The second connector comprises a recessed trajectory for receiving and guiding the protrusion, the recessed trajectory comprising a first trajectory part parallel to the roof trajectory and a second trajectory part having a directional component perpendicular to the roof trajectory, the second trajectory part being connected to the first trajectory part. Sliding roof system according to claim 9, wherein, in operational state, the second trajectory part is facing substantially upward. Sliding roof system according to any of the preceding claims, further comprising a third roof panel slidably mounted to the first roof rail, in closed state of the roof substantially ranging from the first roof panel to the second roof panel, wherein: The panel storage module further comprises a third panel storage position comprising a first storage rail; and The storage driving module is arranged for moving the panel storage module to a fourth storage position in which the first storage rail of the third panel storage position is lined up to the proximal end of the first roof rail. 

 Sliding roof system according to any of the preceding claims, further comprising a second roof rail having a distal end and a proximal end, provided at a rail distance from the first roof rail, the second roof rail being provided substantially parallel to the first roof rail, wherein: The first roof panel is slidably mounted to the second roof rail; The second roof panel slidably mounted to the second roof rail; and Each of the panel storage positions comprises a second storage rail for slidably receiving a roof panel, the second storage rail being provided parallel to and at the rail distance from the first storage rail. Ship comprising: A hull; A forecastle provided in the front of the hull; A front deck; A deck lifting module for lifting the front deck relative to the forecastle; The sliding roof system according to any of the claims 2 to 12 to the extent dependent on claim 2 for covering at least part of the hull; The front deck is arranged to close off the forecastle if the panel storage module is in the third storage position; and The deck lifting module is arranged to lift the front deck prior to the panel storage module moving to a position in which the first storage rail of any of the panel storage positions is aligned with the first roof rail. 

 Ship according to claim 13, wherein the front deck is pivotally mounted to the hull and the pivot axis is located at a front part of the hull. Ship according to claim 13 or 14, wherein: The panel storage module comprises a storage rail abutment for closing off the first roof rail if the panel storage module is in the third position for preventing the first roof panel to slide beyond the proximal end of the first roof rail; and/or The front deck comprises a deck rail abutment for closing off the first roof rail if the front deck is closing off the forecastle for preventing the first roof panel to slide beyond the proximal end of the first roof rail.
